item.title: Former PlayStation boss Shawn Layden wants to save games by bringing back double-A - and he has a plan
item.description:
He's been saying it for years: blockbuster game development is unsustainable today, and given that Shawn Layden used to lead PlayStation's entire roster of first-party studios, he likely knows a fair bit about the issue. "Every generation it costs twice as much to build a game," he told Tom Phillips

Eurogamer — Essay by former PlayStation boss arguing for double-A game development as a solution to industry unsustainability, with specific industry analysis that aligns with the user's demonstrated interest in game design philosophy.
